# Fusion Extension Tools

All tools are registered via the Fusion extension. They are available in any agent session when Fusion is configured.

> Naming contract: all externally exposed Fusion extension tools are `fn_*` (for example `fn_task_create`). Engine runtime sessions also inject additional `fn_*` tools (for example `fn_review_step`, `fn_spawn_agent`, `fn_task_document_write`) that are separate from this extension surface and documented in `engine-tools.md`.

<!-- BEGIN: extension-tools (auto-generated by scripts/sync-fusion-skill-tools.mjs â€” do not edit by hand) -->

## Task Tools

### fn_task_create

Create a new task on the Fusion task board. The task enters the planning column where the AI planning agent will plan it into a full prompt with steps, file scope, and acceptance criteria. Optionally pass workflow_id to select a workflow at creation time; use fn_workflow_list to discover valid IDs.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `description` | string | âś“ | What needs to be done â€” be descriptive |
| `depends` | array | â€” | Task IDs this depends on (e.g. ['FN-001', 'FN-002']) |
| `agentId` | string | â€” | Agent ID to assign this task to (e.g. 'agent-abc123') |
| `priority` | string(enum) | â€” | Task priority (low, normal, high, urgent) |
| `workflow_id` | string | â€” | Workflow ID to select for the new task (e.g. 'WF-003' or 'builtin:coding'). Omit to inherit the project default workflow. Use fn_workflow_list to discover valid IDs. |

### fn_task_update

Update fields on an existing task. Supports modifying the title, description, dependencies, assigned agent, priority, and workflow_id after task creation. Set workflow_id to a workflow ID to select it, or null to clear the workflow selection.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Task ID (e.g. FN-001) |
| `title` | string | â€” | New task title |
| `description` | string | â€” | New task description |
| `depends` | array | â€” | New dependency list â€” replaces existing dependencies (e.g. ['FN-001', 'FN-002']) |
| `agentId` | union | â€” | Agent ID to assign this task to, or null to clear (e.g. 'agent-abc123') |
| `nodeId` | union | â€” | Node ID override for this task, or null to clear |
| `priority` | string(enum) | â€” | Task priority (low, normal, high, urgent) |
| `workflow_id` | union | â€” | Workflow ID to select for this task (e.g. 'WF-003' or 'builtin:coding'), or null to clear the workflow selection and revert to the project default. Use fn_workflow_list to discover valid IDs. |

### fn_task_list

List all tasks on the Fusion board, grouped by column.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `column` | string(enum) | â€” | Filter to a specific column |
| `limit` | number | â€” | Max tasks to show per column (default: 10) |

### fn_task_show

Show full details for a task including steps, progress, and log entries.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Task ID (e.g. FN-001) |

### fn_task_attach

Attach a file to a task. Supports images (png, jpg, gif, webp) and text files (txt, log, json, yaml, yml, toml, csv, xml).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Task ID (e.g. FN-001) |
| `path` | string | âś“ | Path to the file to attach |

### fn_task_pause

Pause a task for explicit user-requested manual control â€” stops all automated agent and scheduler interaction. Agents should not pause tasks to handle failures or blockers; use retry, create/delegate follow-up work, or let the task surface as failed instead.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Task ID (e.g. FN-001) |

### fn_task_unpause

Unpause a task â€” resumes automated agent and scheduler interaction.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Task ID (e.g. FN-001) |

### fn_task_retry

Retry a failed task â€” clears the error state. Non-review failures move to todo; in-review execution failures move to todo preserving progress; in-review merge failures stay in-place for auto-merge retry.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Task ID to retry (e.g. FN-001). Must be in 'failed' state. |

### fn_task_duplicate

Duplicate an existing task, creating a fresh copy in planning. Copies the title and description but resets all execution state. The AI planning agent will replan the new task.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Source task ID to duplicate (e.g. FN-001) |

### fn_task_refine

Request a refinement of a completed or in-review task. Creates a new follow-up task in planning that references the original task as a dependency. Use this when a done or in-review task needs additional work, improvements, or follow-up changes.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Task ID to refine (e.g. FN-001). Must be in 'done' or 'in-review' column. |
| `feedback` | string | âś“ | Description of what needs to be refined or improved |

### fn_task_archive

Archive a task from any live column (move to archived). Archived tasks are preserved for historical reference but moved out of the main board view. If the task is still referenced as a lineage parent by another task, archiving is rejected unless removeLineageReferences:true is passed.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Task ID to archive from any live column (e.g. FN-001). |
| `removeLineageReferences` | boolean | â€” | When true, clear incoming lineage-parent references (child sourceParentTaskId) before archiving, so a task still referenced as a lineage parent can be archived. |

### fn_task_unarchive

Unarchive an archived task (move from archived â†’ its restore column). Restores to the pre-archive column when available, with active execution columns downgraded to todo.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Task ID to unarchive (e.g. FN-001). Must be in 'archived' column. |

### fn_task_delete

Soft-delete a task from active Fusion board views. The task row and artifacts are preserved; optional allowResurrection marks the ID for intentional recreation. If the task is still referenced as a lineage parent by another task, deletion is rejected unless removeLineageReferences:true is passed.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Task ID to delete (e.g. FN-001) |
| `allowResurrection` | boolean | â€” | When true, mark this tombstone as explicitly reusable for future recreation. |
| `removeLineageReferences` | boolean | â€” | When true, clear incoming lineage-parent references (child sourceParentTaskId) before deleting, so a task still referenced as a lineage parent can be removed. |

| `limit` | number | â€” |  |

| `group` | string | âś“ | GitLab group path or numeric ID |
| `limit` | number | â€” |  |
| `labels` | array | â€” |  |

Import GitLab group issues as Fusion tasks using each issue's originating project identity.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `group` | string | âś“ | GitLab group path or numeric ID |
| `limit` | number | â€” |  |
| `labels` | array | â€” |  |

List GitLab project merge requests from the configured GitLab instance.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |

| `project` | string | âś“ | GitLab project path or numeric ID |
| `limit` | number | â€” |  |
| `labels` | array | â€” |  |

Import GitLab project merge requests as Fusion review tasks using configured GitLab HTTP API auth.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `project` | string | âś“ | GitLab project path or numeric ID |
| `limit` | number | â€” |  |
| `labels` | array | â€” |  |

### fn_task_plan

Create a task via AI-guided planning mode â€” interactive conversation to refine your idea into a well-specified task.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `description` | string | â€” | Initial plan description (optional) â€” the AI will ask clarifying questions if not provided |
| `baseBranch` | string | â€” | Optional base branch for the task created from this planning session |

## Workflow Tools

### fn_workflow_list

List built-in and custom Fusion workflow definitions available in this project.

No parameters.

### fn_workflow_get

Fetch a Fusion workflow definition by ID, including its resolved workflow IR.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `workflow_id` | string | âś“ | The workflow definition ID to fetch (e.g. 'WF-003', or a 'builtin:*' id). Use fn_workflow_list to discover available IDs. |

### fn_workflow_create

Create a custom Fusion workflow definition from a validated workflow IR.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | âś“ | Workflow name (required, non-empty). |
| `description` | string | â€” | Optional human-readable description. |
| `icon` | string | â€” | Optional compact plain-text icon for this custom workflow. |
| `ir` | unknown | âś“ | Workflow graph (intermediate representation). Validated server-side; a malformed graph is rejected. |
| `layout` | record | â€” | Optional node layout map keyed by node id. |
| `confirm_policy_escalation` | boolean | â€” | Set true to confirm binding a column to an agent whose permission policy is broader (more privileged) than the project default. Required when such a binding is present; the create is otherwise rejected naming the offending column. |

### fn_workflow_update

Update a custom Fusion workflow definition's metadata, IR, or layout.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `workflow_id` | string | âś“ | The workflow definition ID to update (built-ins cannot be edited). |
| `name` | string | â€” | New name. |
| `description` | string | â€” | New description. |
| `icon` | string | â€” | New compact plain-text icon; blank clears it. |
| `ir` | unknown | â€” | Replacement workflow graph (validated server-side). |
| `layout` | record | â€” | Replacement node layout map. |
| `rehome_to` | string | â€” | When an IR update removes a column that still holds cards, supply the column id to re-home those occupants into. Required to resolve an OccupiedColumns conflict; the target must exist in the new IR. |
| `confirm_policy_escalation` | boolean | â€” | Set true to confirm binding a column to an agent whose permission policy is broader (more privileged) than the project default. Required when such a binding is present; the update is otherwise rejected naming the offending column. |

### fn_workflow_delete

Delete a custom Fusion workflow definition; built-in workflows are protected.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `workflow_id` | string | âś“ | The workflow definition ID to delete (built-ins cannot be deleted). |

### fn_workflow_settings

Read or write per-project values for a workflow's declared settings.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | union | âś“ | `get` reads the stored setting VALUES plus the engine-effective values for the workflow; `set` writes values (requires `values`). |
| `workflow_id` | string | âś“ | The workflow whose setting VALUES to read/write (e.g. 'WF-003', or a 'builtin:*' id). Values are scoped per (workflow, project). Built-in workflow VALUES are writable even though built-in DECLARATIONS are not (declarations are edited via the workflow IR's `settings`). Values are validated against THIS workflow's declared settings (use fn_workflow_get to inspect them). |
| `values` | record | â€” | For action='set': a map of settingId â†’ value to write. A `null` value DELETES the override (null-as-delete). Each value is validated against the named workflow's declaration; on ANY rejection (unknown-setting/type-mismatch/enum-violation/no-settings-defined) nothing is persisted and the typed rejection list is returned. |

### fn_trait_list

List column traits available when authoring Fusion workflow IR columns.

No parameters.

### fn_workflow_select

Assign a workflow definition to a task by workflow ID.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `workflow_id` | string | âś“ | The workflow definition ID to select (e.g. 'WF-003', or a 'builtin:*' id). Use fn_workflow_list to discover available IDs. |
| `task_id` | string | â€” | Task to assign the workflow to. Defaults to the current task. |

## GitHub Tools

### fn_task_import_github

Import GitHub issues as Fusion tasks. Fetches open issues from a repository and creates tasks in the planning column. Each task includes the issue title and body with a link to the source issue.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `ownerRepo` | string | âś“ | Repository in owner/repo format (e.g., 'dustinbyrne/fusion') |
| `limit` | number | â€” | Max issues to import (default: 30, max: 100) |
| `labels` | array | â€” | Label names to filter by |

### fn_task_import_github_issue

Import a specific GitHub issue as a Fusion task. Fetches the issue by number and creates a single task in the planning column with the issue title and body.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `owner` | string | âś“ | Repository owner (e.g., 'dustinbyrne') |
| `repo` | string | âś“ | Repository name (e.g., 'fusion') |
| `issueNumber` | number | âś“ | GitHub issue number to import |

### fn_task_browse_github_issues

List open GitHub issues from a repository to browse before importing. Returns issue numbers, titles, and URLs for selection. Use with fn_task_import_github_issue to import specific issues by number.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `owner` | string | âś“ | Repository owner (e.g., 'dustinbyrne') |
| `repo` | string | âś“ | Repository name (e.g., 'fusion') |
| `limit` | number | â€” | Max issues to show (default: 30, max: 100) |
| `labels` | array | â€” | Label names to filter by |

## Mission Tools

### fn_mission_create

Create a new mission â€” a high-level objective that can span multiple milestones. Missions contain milestones that break down work into phases.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `title` | string | âś“ | Mission title â€” brief but descriptive |
| `description` | string | â€” | Detailed mission objectives and context |
| `autoAdvance` | boolean | â€” | Automatically activate the next pending slice when the current slice completes |
| `baseBranch` | string | â€” | Optional integration base branch for tasks triaged from this mission |

### fn_mission_list

List all missions with their current status.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `includeDrafts` | boolean | â€” | Include in-flight mission interview drafts (default: true) |

### fn_mission_show

Show mission details with full hierarchy: milestones â†’ slices â†’ features.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Mission ID (e.g., M-001) |

### fn_mission_list_goals

List goals linked to a mission.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `missionId` | string | âś“ | Mission ID (e.g., M-001) |

### fn_mission_link_goal

Link a goal to a mission.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `missionId` | string | âś“ | Mission ID (e.g., M-001) |
| `goalId` | string | âś“ | Goal ID (e.g., G-001) |

### fn_mission_unlink_goal

Unlink a goal from a mission.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `missionId` | string | âś“ | Mission ID (e.g., M-001) |
| `goalId` | string | âś“ | Goal ID (e.g., G-001) |

### fn_mission_backfill_assertions

Backfill mission assertions by deriving and linking one store-managed assertion for each feature without linked assertions. Supports dry-run mode.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `missionId` | string | â€” | Mission ID to scope backfill to (e.g., M-001) |
| `dryRun` | boolean | â€” | When true, preview repairs without writing changes (default: true) |

### fn_mission_delete

Delete a mission and all its milestones, slices, and features. Cannot be undone.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Mission ID to delete (e.g., M-001) |

### fn_mission_update

Update an existing mission's title or description. Partial patches leave untouched fields intact.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Mission ID to update (e.g., M-001) |
| `title` | string | â€” | Updated mission title |
| `description` | string | â€” | Updated mission description |

### fn_milestone_add

Add a milestone to a mission. Milestones represent phases of work.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `missionId` | string | âś“ | Parent mission ID (e.g., M-001) |
| `title` | string | âś“ | Milestone title |
| `description` | string | â€” | Milestone description |

### fn_slice_add

Add a slice to a milestone. Slices are work units that can be activated for implementation.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `milestoneId` | string | âś“ | Parent milestone ID (e.g., MS-001) |
| `title` | string | âś“ | Slice title |
| `description` | string | â€” | Slice description |

### fn_feature_add

Add a feature to a slice. Features are deliverables that can be linked to tasks.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `sliceId` | string | âś“ | Parent slice ID (e.g., SL-001) |
| `title` | string | âś“ | Feature title |
| `description` | string | â€” | Feature description |
| `acceptanceCriteria` | string | â€” | Acceptance criteria for completing the feature |

### fn_feature_delete

Delete a feature. Rejects deletion when linked to a live task unless force=true.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `featureId` | string | âś“ | Feature ID to delete (e.g., F-001) |
| `force` | boolean | â€” | Override linked-task guard |

### fn_slice_delete

Delete a slice and its features. Rejects deletion when child features link to live tasks unless force=true.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `sliceId` | string | âś“ | Slice ID to delete (e.g., SL-001) |
| `force` | boolean | â€” | Override linked-task guard |

### fn_milestone_delete

Delete a milestone and all descendant slices/features. Rejects deletion when child features link to live tasks unless force=true.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `milestoneId` | string | âś“ | Milestone ID to delete (e.g., MS-001) |
| `force` | boolean | â€” | Override linked-task guard |

### fn_slice_activate

Activate a pending slice for implementation. Sets status to 'active' and enables task linking for its features.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Slice ID to activate (e.g., SL-001) |

### fn_feature_link_task

Link a feature to a fn task for implementation. Updates the feature status to 'triaged' and associates it with the task. If the target task is not on the active board (for example archived, deleted, or never created), the tool returns a clear validation error indicating that only active tasks can be linked.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `featureId` | string | âś“ | Feature ID to link (e.g., F-001) |
| `taskId` | string | âś“ | Task ID to link to (e.g., FN-001) |

### fn_feature_update

Update an existing feature's title, description, or acceptance criteria. Partial patches leave untouched fields intact.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Feature ID to update (e.g., F-001) |
| `title` | string | â€” | Updated feature title |
| `description` | string | â€” | Updated feature description |
| `acceptanceCriteria` | string | â€” | Updated acceptance criteria for completing the feature |

### fn_milestone_update

Update an existing milestone's title, description, or acceptance criteria (the structured pass/fail bar, distinct from verification's free-form how-to-confirm notes). Partial patches leave untouched fields intact.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Milestone ID to update (e.g., MS-001) |
| `title` | string | â€” | Updated milestone title |
| `description` | string | â€” | Updated milestone description |
| `acceptanceCriteria` | string | â€” | Updated acceptance criteria for completing the milestone |

## Goal Tools

### fn_goal_list

List goals by status with active-goal warning details.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `status` | union | â€” | Filter by goal status (default: active) |

### fn_goal_create

Create a new project goal.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `title` | string | âś“ | Goal title â€” brief but descriptive |
| `description` | string | â€” | Long-form goal description (free-text markdown) |

### fn_goal_archive

Archive a goal by ID.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Goal ID (G-â€¦) to archive |

### fn_goal_show

Show full details for a single goal by ID.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Goal ID (G-â€¦) |

## Agent Tools

### fn_agent_stop

Stop a running agent â€” pauses its execution. Transitions the agent from running/active to paused state.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Agent ID to stop (e.g., agent-abc123) |

### fn_agent_start

Start a stopped agent â€” resumes its execution. Transitions the agent from paused to active state.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Agent ID to start (e.g., agent-abc123) |

### fn_agent_create

Create a new non-ephemeral agent.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | âś“ | Agent name |
| `role` | union | âś“ | Agent role/capability |
| `soul` | string | â€” | Agent personality/identity text |
| `instructions_text` | string | â€” | Inline custom instructions |
| `instructions_path` | string | â€” | Path to instructions markdown |
| `reportsTo` | string | â€” | Manager agent ID |
| `heartbeat_interval_ms` | number | â€” |  |
| `heartbeat_timeout_ms` | number | â€” |  |
| `max_concurrent_runs` | number | â€” |  |
| `message_response_mode` | union | â€” |  |

### fn_agent_update

Update editable configuration for an existing non-ephemeral agent. Agent callers can only update direct or indirect reports inside their management subtree; user/operator calls are privileged.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `agent_id` | string | âś“ | Target agent ID or name to update |
| `name` | string | â€” | New display name |
| `role` | union | â€” | Agent role/capability |
| `title` | string | â€” | Optional title shown for the agent |
| `icon` | string | â€” | Optional compact icon/emoji |
| `soul` | string | â€” | Agent personality/identity text |
| `instructions_text` | string | â€” | Inline custom instructions |
| `instructions_path` | string | â€” | Path to instructions markdown |
| `heartbeat_procedure_path` | string | â€” | Path to heartbeat procedure markdown |
| `reportsTo` | string | â€” | Manager agent ID/name. Pass empty string to clear for privileged user/operator calls. |
| `heartbeat_interval_ms` | number | â€” | Heartbeat polling interval in ms |
| `heartbeat_timeout_ms` | number | â€” | Heartbeat timeout in ms |
| `max_concurrent_runs` | number | â€” | Max concurrent heartbeat runs |
| `message_response_mode` | union | â€” | How agent responds to messages |

### fn_agent_set_instructions

Set the instructionsText and/or instructionsPath of one of the caller's direct or indirect reports. At least one of instructions_text or instructions_path is required; pass an empty string to clear a field. The change is persisted and recorded as a config revision.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `agent_id` | string | âś“ | Target agent whose instructions to set |
| `instructions_text` | string | â€” | Inline instructions. Pass an empty string to clear. |
| `instructions_path` | string | â€” | Path to a markdown instructions file. Pass an empty string to clear. |

### fn_agent_delete

Delete a non-ephemeral agent.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `agent_id` | string | âś“ | Agent ID to delete |
| `force` | boolean | â€” | Force delete when holding checkout |
| `reassign_to` | string | â€” | Optional replacement agent for assigned tasks |

### fn_list_agents

List all available agents in the system. Shows each agent's name, role, state, personality (soul), and current assignment. Use this to discover which agents exist and what they specialize in before delegating work.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `role` | string | â€” | Filter by agent role/capability (e.g., 'executor', 'reviewer', 'qa') |
| `state` | string | â€” | Filter by agent state (e.g., 'idle', 'active', 'running') |
| `includeEphemeral` | boolean | â€” | Include ephemeral/runtime agents (default: false) |

### fn_delegate_task

Create a new task and assign it to a specific agent for execution. The task goes to 'todo' and will be picked up by the target agent on their next heartbeat cycle. Use fn_list_agents first to find available agents and their capabilities. Optionally pass workflow_id to select a workflow at creation time; use fn_workflow_list to discover valid IDs.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `agent_id` | string | âś“ | The agent ID to delegate work to |
| `description` | string | âś“ | What needs to be done |
| `dependencies` | array | â€” | Task IDs this new task depends on (e.g. ["KB-001"] |
| `workflow_id` | string | â€” | Workflow ID to select for the new task (e.g. 'WF-003' or 'builtin:coding'). Omit to inherit the project default workflow. Use fn_workflow_list to discover valid IDs. |
| `override` | boolean | â€” | Set true to bypass executor-role assignment policy |

### fn_agent_show

Show detailed information about a single agent, including their role, state, position in the org hierarchy (reports-to, direct reports), skills, and current assignment.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Agent ID or resolvable name |

### fn_agent_org_chart

Show the organizational tree of agents, displaying the role hierarchy. Optionally filter to a subtree rooted at a specific agent.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `root_agent_id` | string | â€” | If provided, show only the subtree rooted at this agent |
| `include_ephemeral` | boolean | â€” | Include ephemeral/runtime agents (default: false) |

## Skills Tools

### fn_skills_search

Search the skills.sh directory for agent skills. Returns matching skills with names, sources (owner/repo), install counts, and install commands. Use fn_skills_install to install a selected skill.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `query` | string | âś“ | Search query â€” framework name, technology, or capability (e.g., 'react', 'firebase', 'testing', 'docker') |
| `limit` | number | â€” | Max results to return (default: 10, max: 50) |

### fn_skills_install

Install an agent skill from skills.sh into the current project. Downloads skill files into the project's skill directories (.fusion/skills/, legacy .pi/skills/, .agents/skills/). The skill becomes available to AI agents in subsequent sessions.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `source` | string | âś“ | GitHub source in owner/repo format (e.g., 'firebase/agent-skills') |
| `skill` | string | â€” | Specific skill name to install (e.g., 'firebase-basics'). Omit to install all skills from the source. |

## Insight Tools

### fn_insight_list

List persisted project insights with optional category/status filters.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `category` | string(enum) | â€” | Filter by insight category |
| `status` | string(enum) | â€” | Filter by insight status |
| `runId` | string | â€” | Filter to insights linked to a specific run ID |
| `limit` | number | â€” | Max insights to return |
| `offset` | number | â€” | Number of rows to skip |

### fn_insight_show

Show a single persisted insight by ID.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Insight ID (e.g. INS-XXXXX) |

### fn_insight_run_list

List recent insight-generation runs with optional status/trigger filters.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `status` | string(enum) | â€” | Filter by run status |
| `trigger` | string(enum) | â€” | Filter by run trigger |
| `limit` | number | â€” | Max runs to return |
| `offset` | number | â€” | Number of runs to skip |

### fn_insight_run_show

Show a single insight-generation run by ID.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Insight run ID (e.g. INSR-XXXXX) |

## Other Tools

### fn_web_fetch

Lightweight URL fetch (no JS rendering). Use agent-browser skill for JS-heavy pages. URL to fetch (http/https) Optional extraction hint for downstream summarization Timeout in milliseconds (default: 30000) Max bytes to return (default: 512000)

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `url` | string | âś“ | URL to fetch (http/https) |
| `prompt` | string | â€” | Optional extraction hint for downstream summarization |
| `timeoutMs` | number | â€” | Timeout in milliseconds (default: 30000) |
| `maxBytes` | number | â€” | Max bytes to return (default: 512000) |

### fn_secret_get

Read a secret by key using per-secret access policy.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `key` | string | âś“ | Secret key |
| `scope` | union | â€” | Optional scope |

### fn_research_run

Cited-research pipeline: create a bounded search/fetch/synthesis run (not an autonomous experiment loop) and optionally wait for completion.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `query` | string | âś“ | Research query or question |
| `wait_for_completion` | boolean | â€” | Wait for the run to complete before returning (default: false) |
| `max_wait_ms` | number | â€” | Max wait time when wait_for_completion=true (default: 90000, capped by settings) |

### fn_research_list

Cited-research pipeline: list recent search/fetch/synthesis runs (not experiment-loop sessions).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `status` | string(enum) | â€” | Filter by run status |
| `limit` | number | â€” | Max runs to return (default: 10) |

### fn_research_get

Cited-research pipeline: get one run with structured findings and citations (not experiment-loop state).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Research run ID |

### fn_research_cancel

Cited-research pipeline: cancel an in-flight run; terminal runs return INVALID_TRANSITION (does not control experiment loops).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Research run ID |

### fn_research_retry

Cited-research pipeline: retry a failed run when lifecycle marks it retryable (not an autonomous experiment loop retry).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | âś“ | Research run ID |

### fn_experiment_finalize

Group kept experiment runs into reviewable branches and finalize the session. Use dryRun=true to preview the plan without touching git.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `sessionId` | string | âś“ | Experiment session ID |
| `integrationBranch` | string | â€” | Integration branch to compute merge-base against (default: main) |
| `dryRun` | boolean | â€” | Preview plan only; do not create branches |
| `planOverride` | unknown | â€” | Optional plan override payload |
| `summary` | string | â€” | Optional finalize summary |

<!-- END: extension-tools -->

## Goal retrieval contract notes

### Goal-show response contract

- **Success:** returns `details.goal` as the complete goal JSON object (`id`, `title`, optional `description`, `status`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`) and `content[0].text` with those same human-readable fields.
- **Not found:** returns `isError: true` with `details.code: "GOAL_NOT_FOUND"` and `details.goalId` set to the requested id.

## Dashboard Command

### /fn

Start or stop the Fusion dashboard from within an agent session.

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/fn` | Start dashboard on port 4040 |
| `/fn 8080` | Start on custom port |
| `/fn stop` | Stop dashboard |
| `/fn status` | Check if running |
